understanding.goal_understanding: The objective is to complete each level by positioning all selectable pieces onto goal tiles (marked '+'). If multiple goal tiles exist, every one must be occupied simultaneously to win the level. The overall game is won when all levels are solved within their allotted step budgets.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Movement occurs on a pixel grid with 3 pixels per cell. The player controls one selectable piece at a time, issuing MOVE actions in four directions. Walls and boundaries may block movement, but the 'movement_{direction}_blocked' hint is only a heuristic. Some objects ('blocks') are pushable, allowing the selected piece to displace them if adjacent. Control can switch to another selectable piece using a CLICK action with its exact pixel coordinates. Each level has a finite step budget, enforcing efficient puzzle solving.

understanding.goal_understanding: The objective is to complete each level by positioning all selectable pieces onto goal tiles (marked '+'). If multiple goal tiles exist, every one must be occupied simultaneously to win the level. The overall game is won when all levels are solved within their allotted step budgets.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Movement occurs on a pixel grid with 3 pixels per cell. The player controls one selectable piece at a time, issuing MOVE actions in four directions. Walls and boundaries may block movement, but the 'movement_{direction}_blocked' hint is only a heuristic. Some objects ('blocks') are pushable, allowing the selected piece to displace them if adjacent. Control can switch to another selectable piece using a CLICK action with its exact pixel coordinates. Each level has a finite step budget, enforcing efficient puzzle solving.
